annotations.label: Bernoulli process

anchor.snippet: a Bernoulli process (named after Jacob Bernoulli ) is a finite or infinite sequence of binary random variables

annotations.note: Mathlib has Bernoulli-distributed random variables (IsSetBernoulli, PMF.bernoulli, bernoulliMeasure) but no named decl for the full sequence-of-iid-Bernoulli `Bernoulli process` object.

annotations.label: Bernoulli scheme (generalization)

anchor.snippet: this generalization is known as the Bernoulli scheme

annotations.note: No Mathlib decl for the Bernoulli scheme (i.i.d. categorical/multinomial sequence) was found.

annotations.label: Bernoulli process (formal sequence definition)

anchor.snippet: A Bernoulli process is a finite or infinite sequence of independent random variables

annotations.note: Independence of a sequence is captured by iIndepFun, and individual Bernoulli laws by PMF.bernoulli, but their combination as a `Bernoulli process` is not named.

annotations.label: Memorylessness of Bernoulli process

anchor.snippet: Independence of the trials implies that the process is memoryless

annotations.note: No specific memorylessness statement for a Bernoulli process is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Fresh-start property

anchor.snippet: the future trials constitute a Bernoulli process identical to the whole process, the fresh-start property

annotations.note: No `fresh-start`/strong-Markov shift lemma for a Bernoulli sequence appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bernoulli trial with parameter p

anchor.snippet: the individual variables X i may be called Bernoulli trials with parameter p

annotations.note: A single Bernoulli trial with parameter p is exactly PMF.bernoulli p (or ProbabilityTheory.bernoulliMeasure).

annotations.label: Derived distributions from Bernoulli process

anchor.snippet: Several random variables and probability distributions beside the Bernoullis may be derived from the Bernoulli process

annotations.note: This is a meta-remark about derived laws, not a single formalizable statement.

annotations.label: Formalization via probability spaces

anchor.snippet: The Bernoulli process can be formalized in the language of probability spaces as a random sequence of independent realisations of a random variable

annotations.note: Mathlib has the iIndepFun probability-space framework and a Bernoulli law, but does not package them as a Bernoulli-process probability triple.

annotations.label: Cylinder sets and Borel algebra

anchor.snippet: These sets of finite sequences are referred to as cylinder sets in the product topology

annotations.note: Cylinder sets generating the product σ-algebra are defined as MeasureTheory.cylinder with generateFrom_measurableCylinders.

annotations.label: Bernoulli distribution Ber(p)

anchor.snippet: if a discrete random variable X has a Bernoulli distribution with parameter p

annotations.note: Ber(p) is bernoulliMeasure with notation `Ber(x, y, p)`, also available as PMF.bernoulli.

annotations.label: Bernoulli measure

anchor.snippet: is commonly called the Bernoulli measure

annotations.note: The single-trial bernoulliMeasure exists; the product Bernoulli measure on 2^ℕ is not given by a dedicated decl.

annotations.label: Probability of a specific infinite sequence is zero

anchor.snippet: the probability of any specific, infinitely long sequence of coin flips is exactly zero

annotations.note: No Mathlib decl asserts the product-Bernoulli measure of a singleton in 2^ℕ is 0.

annotations.label: Bernoulli process as probability triple

anchor.snippet: a Bernoulli process is then given by the probability triple

annotations.note: Mathlib does not package the (Ω, Σ, P) triple for the Bernoulli process as a named object.

annotations.label: Law of large numbers for Bernoulli process

anchor.section: Law of large numbers, binomial distribution and central limit theorem
anchor.snippet: The law of large numbers states that the average of the sequence

annotations.note: The strong law of large numbers for i.i.d. integrable variables (strong_law_ae) specializes to the Bernoulli case.

annotations.label: Counting strings with k heads via binomial coefficient

anchor.section: Law of large numbers, binomial distribution and central limit theorem
anchor.snippet: the number N ( k , n ) of such strings that contain k occurrences of H is given by the binomial coefficient

annotations.note: The count of k-out-of-n binary strings is Nat.choose n k, established via Finset.card_powersetCard and related combinatorics.

annotations.label: Binomial distribution

anchor.section: Law of large numbers, binomial distribution and central limit theorem
anchor.snippet: The probability measure thus defined is known as the Binomial distribution

annotations.note: ProbabilityTheory.binomial (and PMF.binomial) define the binomial distribution Bin(n, p).

annotations.label: Bernoulli distribution as special case of binomial

anchor.section: Law of large numbers, binomial distribution and central limit theorem
anchor.snippet: the Bernoulli distribution is exactly a special case of Binomial distribution when n equals to 1

annotations.note: Loogle finds binomial_one_eq_bernoulliMeasure (and PMF.binomial_one_eq_bernoulli) stating Bin(1, p) = Ber(p).

annotations.label: Central limit theorem for Bernoulli process

anchor.section: Law of large numbers, binomial distribution and central limit theorem
anchor.snippet: one obtains the Normal distribution ; this is the content of the central limit theorem

annotations.note: Mathlib has the 1-D CLT for i.i.d. variables, which applies to Bernoulli sequences.

annotations.label: Asymptotic equipartition property

anchor.section: Law of large numbers, binomial distribution and central limit theorem
anchor.snippet: the asymptotic equipartition property

annotations.note: No Shannon-McMillan/AEP statement appears in Mathlib (equipartition hits are unrelated graph-regularity decls).

annotations.label: Kolmogorov 0-1 law

anchor.section: Law of large numbers, binomial distribution and central limit theorem
anchor.snippet: This partitioning is known as the Kolmogorov 0-1 law

annotations.note: Kolmogorov's 0-1 law for the tail σ-algebra of an independent sequence is in Probability.Independence.ZeroOne.

annotations.label: Bernoulli entropy

anchor.section: Law of large numbers, binomial distribution and central limit theorem
anchor.snippet: This value is the Bernoulli entropy of a Bernoulli process

annotations.note: No binary/Bernoulli entropy function -p log p - (1-p) log (1-p) is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ornstein isomorphism theorem

anchor.section: Law of large numbers, binomial distribution and central limit theorem
anchor.snippet: was finally and completely answered with the Ornstein isomorphism theorem

annotations.note: Ornstein's isomorphism theorem for Bernoulli shifts is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Shift operator on product space

anchor.snippet: There is a natural translation symmetry on the product space

annotations.note: No Bernoulli-shift / product-space translation operator decl was found.

annotations.label: Translation-invariance of Bernoulli measure (Haar measure)

anchor.snippet: The Bernoulli measure, defined above, is translation-invariant

annotations.note: Mathlib has IsHaarMeasure abstractly but no decl asserting the product Bernoulli measure is shift-invariant.

annotations.label: Transfer (Ruelle–Frobenius–Perron) operator

anchor.snippet: This linear operator is called the transfer operator or the Ruelle–Frobenius–Perron operator

annotations.note: No transfer/Ruelle/Frobenius–Perron operator is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Frobenius–Perron eigenvalue and Bernoulli-measure eigenvector

anchor.snippet: The largest eigenvalue is the Frobenius–Perron eigenvalue

annotations.note: No Frobenius–Perron spectral statement for the transfer operator appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bernoulli polynomials as eigenfunctions on polynomials

anchor.snippet: the eigenfunctions are (curiously) the Bernoulli polynomials

annotations.note: Polynomial.bernoulli is defined and studied, but no result identifies them as eigenfunctions of a transfer/dyadic operator.

annotations.label: Dyadic transformation

anchor.snippet: This map is called the dyadic transformation

annotations.note: The map x ↦ 2x mod 1 is not defined as a named decl in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Baker's map

anchor.snippet: the induced homomorphism is the Baker's map

annotations.note: No Baker's map decl was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Discrete spectrum on polynomials via Bernoulli polynomials

anchor.snippet: Restricting the action of the operator

annotations.note: No spectral statement for the dyadic transfer operator on polynomials exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cantor function from binary sum

anchor.snippet: gives the Cantor function , as conventionally defined

annotations.note: Cardinal.cantorFunction c f = Σ f n · c^n implements this binary-sum Cantor function.

annotations.label: Cantor set identification

anchor.snippet: is sometimes called the Cantor set

annotations.note: The ternary Cantor set is defined as cantorSet := ⋂ n, preCantorSet n in Topology.Instances.CantorSet.

annotations.label: Odometer dynamical system

anchor.snippet: Another way to create a dynamical system is to define an odometer

annotations.note: No odometer dynamical system decl was found in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Odometer preserves Bernoulli measure only for fair coin

anchor.snippet: It leaves the Bernoulli measure invariant only for the special case

annotations.note: No statement about odometer invariance of the Bernoulli measure exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bernoulli sequence

anchor.snippet: called the Bernoulli sequence

annotations.note: No `Bernoulli sequence`-as-random-subset-of-ℕ decl was found.

annotations.label: Bernoulli sequence as random subset of natural numbers

anchor.snippet: is also a random subset of the index set, the natural numbers

annotations.note: No Mathlib decl identifies a Bernoulli sequence with a random subset of ℕ.

annotations.label: Almost all Bernoulli sequences are ergodic

anchor.snippet: Almost all Bernoulli sequences

annotations.note: No ergodicity-almost-everywhere result for Bernoulli sequences is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Von Neumann randomness extractor

anchor.snippet: by the von Neumann extractor , the earliest randomness extractor

annotations.note: No randomness extractor (von Neumann or otherwise) is defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Basic von Neumann extractor algorithm

anchor.snippet: group that input stream in non-overlapping pairs of successive bits

annotations.note: The von Neumann pair-grouping extraction algorithm is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Worked example of von Neumann extraction

anchor.snippet: an input stream of eight bits 10011011 would by grouped into pairs

annotations.note: Worked examples of the von Neumann extractor are not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Output bits of von Neumann extractor are equally likely

anchor.snippet: In the output stream 0 and 1 are equally likely

annotations.note: No statement about the output distribution of the von Neumann extractor exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Average discard proportion of von Neumann extractor

anchor.snippet: On average the computation discards proportion

annotations.note: No discard-rate computation for the von Neumann extractor is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Iterated von Neumann extractor (AMLS)

anchor.snippet: The iterated version of the von Neumann algorithm, also known as advanced multi-level strategy (AMLS)

annotations.note: The iterated/AMLS extractor is not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: AMLS worked example on 11001011101110

anchor.snippet: The input stream from the AMLS paper

annotations.note: Worked AMLS examples are not in Mathlib.
